remember: Students will be able to define the different types of ocean movements.
Students will be able to recall the factors influencing ocean currents and their characteristics.
Students will be able to identify the major ocean currents and their directions.
understand: Students will be able to explain the formation of ocean currents and waves.
Students will be able to describe the role of winds, tides, and thermohaline circulation in ocean water movement.
Students will be able to differentiate between warm and cold ocean currents.
apply: Students will be able to use the knowledge of ocean currents to predict coastal erosion and deposition.
Students will be able to apply the concept of ocean movements to understand the distribution of marine life.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of ocean currents on climate and navigation.
analyze: Students will be able to compare and contrast the characteristics of different ocean currents.
Students will be able to evaluate the significance of ocean movements in the global energy balance.
Students will be able to discuss the human impact on ocean currents and the consequences of these impacts.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1, to understand the concept of ocean currents and their importance.
To comprehend the factors that influence the movement of ocean water, as outlined in the NCERT syllabus.
To learn about the types and characteristics of ocean currents, as specified in the NCERT textbook.
board_exam_objectives: CBSE tests the ability to define and explain key terms related to ocean movements.
The board exam assesses the understanding of the factors influencing ocean currents and their effects on the environment.
Students are expected to apply their knowledge of ocean currents to real-world scenarios and case studies.
